statement.p:
Determine whether each quadratic graph opens upward or downward.
The graph of y=3x^2-4x-7 opens
?
upward
downward .
The graph of y=-5x^2+x opens
?
upward
downward .
The graph of y=2+3x-x^2 opens
?
upward
downward .
The graph of y=\frac{1}{3}x^2-\frac{2}{5}x+\frac{1}{4} opens
?
upward
downward .

li.p: The graph of y=3x^2-4x-7 opens upward as the leading coefficient is the positive number 3\text{.}
The graph of y=-5x^2+x opens downward as the leading coefficient is the negative number -5\text{.}
The graph of y=2+3x-x^2 opens downward as the leading coefficient is -1\text{.} (Note that the leading coefficient is the coefficient on x^2\text{.})
The graph of y=\frac{1}{3}x^2-\frac{2}{5}x+\frac{1}{4} opens upward as the leading coefficient is the positive number \frac{1}{3}\text{.}

webwork-reps.pg:
############################################# ### Generated from PreTeXt source ### ### https://pretextbook.org ### ############################################# ## DBsubject() ## DBchapter() ## DBsection() ## Level() ## KEYWORDS() ## TitleText1() ## EditionText1() ## AuthorText1() ## Section1(not reported) ## Problem1(1.6.8) ## Author() ## Institution() ## Language(en-US)
DOCUMENT();
############################################################ # Load Macros ############################################################ loadMacros( "PGstandard.pl", "PGML.pl", "AnswerFormatHelp.pl", "parserPopUp.pl", "PGcourse.pl", ); COMMENT('Authored in PreTeXt');
############################################################ # Header ############################################################ TEXT(beginproblem());
############################################################ # PG Setup Code ############################################################ Context('Numeric'); $up = PopUp(['?','upward','downward'],1); $down = PopUp(['?','upward','downward'],2);
############################################################ # Body ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML Determine whether each quadratic graph opens upward or downward. a. The graph of [`y=3x^2-4x-7`] opens [_]{$up}{5}.
a. The graph of [`y=-5x^2+x`] opens [_]{$down}{5}.
a. The graph of [`y=2+3x-x^2`] opens [_]{$down}{5}.
a. The graph of [`y=\frac{1}{3}x^2-\frac{2}{5}x+\frac{1}{4}`] opens [_]{$up}{5}.
END_PGML
############################################################ # Solution ############################################################
BEGIN_PGML_SOLUTION a. The graph of [`y=3x^2-4x-7`] opens upward as the leading coefficient is the positive number [`3\text{.}`]
a. The graph of [`y=-5x^2+x`] opens downward as the leading coefficient is the negative number [`-5\text{.}`]
a. The graph of [`y=2+3x-x^2`] opens downward as the leading coefficient is [`-1\text{.}`] (Note that the leading coefficient is the coefficient on [`x^2\text{.}`])
a. The graph of [`y=\frac{1}{3}x^2-\frac{2}{5}x+\frac{1}{4}`] opens upward as the leading coefficient is the positive number [`\frac{1}{3}\text{.}`]
END_PGML_SOLUTION
############################################################ # End Problem ############################################################
ENDDOCUMENT();
